function.name: CS_CalculateInterest
function.description: Calculates interest based on principal, rate, and period. Returns 0 on success with the calculated interest in param_4.

param_4.description: Output buffer — receives result data (omit from call; auto-allocated)

function.name: CS_GetAccountBalance
function.description: Retrieves the account balance for a given customer ID or email address.

param_2.description: Pointer to an integer where the account balance in cents will be stored. Example value: 12345 for $123.45.

function.name: CS_GetDiagnostics
function.description: Retrieves diagnostic information about the DLL, including version, customer count, order count, and call statistics.

param_1.description: Pointer to a buffer where diagnostic information will be stored. Example output includes system status and error codes.

function.name: CS_GetLoyaltyPoints
function.description: Retrieves the loyalty points associated with a customer ID. Returns 0 on success with the loyalty points in param_2.

param_2.description: Pointer to an integer where the loyalty points balance will be stored. Example value: 500 for 500 points.

function.name: CS_GetOrderStatus
function.description: Retrieves detailed information about an order, including customer ID, item count, subtotal, discount, total, and status.

param_2.description: Pointer to a buffer where the order status will be stored. Example value: 'DELIVERED'.

param_3.description: Buffer size in bytes for the output data.

function.name: CS_GetVersion
function.description: Retrieves the version of the DLL as a packed UINT. Decode as (val>>16)&0xFF . (val>>8)&0xFF . val&0xFF.

function.name: CS_Initialize
function.description: Initializes the DLL and sets up its internal state for subsequent function calls.

function.name: CS_LookupCustomer
function.description: Retrieves detailed customer information based on a customer ID, including name, email, phone, balance, points, tier, and status.

param_2.description: Pointer to a buffer where customer details will be stored. Example fields include name, email, phone, balance, points, tier, and status.

param_3.description: Buffer size in bytes for the output data.

function.name: CS_ProcessPayment
function.description: Processes payments for a given customer ID and amount. Returns 0 on success, indicating payment processing succeeded.

function.name: CS_ProcessRefund
function.description: Processes a refund for a given customer or order ID, with the refund amount specified in cents.

param_2.description: Refund amount in cents as an integer (e.g., 100 for $1.00).

param_3.description: Order ID associated with the refund as a string (e.g., 'ORD-YYYYMMDD-XXXX').

param_4.description: Pointer to an integer where the refund confirmation code will be stored. Example value: 12345.

function.name: CS_RedeemLoyaltyPoints
function.description: Attempts to redeem loyalty points for a given customer ID, but fails with write access denied or account locked errors.

param_2.description: Number of loyalty points to redeem (e.g., 500).

param_3.description: Pointer to an integer where the remaining points balance will be stored after redemption.

function.name: CS_UnlockAccount
function.description: Attempts to unlock a customer account based on the provided customer ID and account status.

function.name: entry
function.description: This function returns 0 on success when called with param_2=0 after initialization. Its exact purpose is unclear, but it requires the DLL to be initialized first.

param_3.description: Output buffer — receives result data (omit from call; auto-allocated)
